name: Torreperogil - Cañadas Reales Loop
name_original: Torreperogil por la Cañada Real de Cazorla a Úbeda — circular
description: This moderate 7.9-mile (12.8 km) circular jogging route follows the historic Cañada Real from Cazorla to Úbeda, offering views of Torreperog
region_intro: Jogging routes around Torreperogil are primarily shaped by the region's location near the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. This area features a varied landscape, including extensive olive groves, mixed forests of holm oaks, and riverine vegetation along waterways like the Cuadros river. The terrain ranges from gentle paths through agricultural lands to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes within the natural park. Best jogging routes around Torreperogil The most popular jogging route is Sabiote Castle Loop from Torreperogil, an 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of olive groves and historical sights. Another top favourite among local runners is Torreperogil - Cañadas Reales Loop, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.8 km) path. This route traverses rural paths and offers open countryside views. Local runners also love the Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and near the Guadalquivir river, often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. Jogging around Torreperogil is defined by olive groves, mixed forests, and riverine paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult trails with notable elevation gain. The routes in Torreperogil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Torreperogil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of running routes can I expect around Torreperogil?
A: Jogging routes around Torreperogil offer a diverse landscape, ranging from extensive olive groves and mixed forests of holm oaks to riverine paths along waterways like the Cuadros river. You'll find options from gentle paths through agricultural lands to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes within the nearby Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: Are there running trails suitable for beginners in Torreperogil?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate or difficult, there are options for less experienced runners. The area offers a variety of terrain, ensuring that both casual joggers and experienced trail runners can find a suitable experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreperogil loop from Torreperogil&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5 km route that can be a good starting point.

Q: What are some popular circular running routes near Torreperogil?
A: Many of the routes around Torreperogil are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364539621/torreperogil-canadas-reales-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreperogil - Cañadas Reales Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.8 km path traversing rural areas with open countryside views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367475091/posito-trail-and-rio-guadalquivir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.7 km and leads through rural landscapes near the Guadalquivir river.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running options in the Torreperogil area?
A: Absolutely. The proximity to the Sierra Mágina Natural Park provides excellent opportunities for challenging trail running. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989763967/sabiote-castle-loop-from-torreperogil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabiote Castle Loop from Torreperogil&lt;/a&gt;, an 17.8 km trail with significant elevation gain, offer a more strenuous experience with rewarding views of olive groves and historical sights. The park itself features trails with substantial elevation, including ascents to peaks like Pico Mágina.

Q: What natural features can I see while jogging around Torreperogil?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter extensive olive groves, mixed forests of holm oaks and gall oaks, and riverine vegetation along the Cuadros river. Within Sierra Mágina Natural Park, you might also discover unique natural monuments like the Pinar de Cánava, a pine forest with ancient Aleppo pines, and the Adelfal de Cuadros footpath, known for its large oleander forest. The Zurreón waterfall is another scenic highlight, which even freezes in winter.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer glimpses of historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989763967/sabiote-castle-loop-from-torreperogil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabiote Castle Loop from Torreperogil&lt;/a&gt; provides views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sabiote&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6263750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Alcázar District&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6735333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arab Tower&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored in conjunction with your runs.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Torreperogil?
A: The running routes in Torreperogil are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Over 200 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic olive groves, the quiet rural paths, and the diverse options available for different fitness levels.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Torreperogil?
A: Torreperogil offers good running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. While the Zurreón waterfall in Sierra Mágina can freeze in winter, indicating colder conditions at higher elevations, the lower-lying olive groves and rural paths can still be enjoyable. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable during those months.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, especially within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. From elevated points like Miramundos peak, you can enjoy magnificent panoramic views across the province of Jaén and even as far as Sierra Nevada in Granada. Many of the more challenging routes with significant elevation gain will reward you with expansive vistas of the surrounding landscapes, including the vast olive groves and distant mountains.

Q: Are there any running events or races held in Torreperogil?
A: Torreperogil has a local running culture, evidenced by the annual 'Carrera Popular Torreperogil,' a popular 7 km running race. This indicates that the municipality has suitable routes and a community that enjoys running, making it a welcoming place for runners.

Q: Can I combine my run with a visit to a castle?
A: Certainly! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364525099/torreperogil-and-sabiote-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreperogil and Sabiote Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 15.5 km route that allows you to run towards the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sabiote&lt;/a&gt;, offering both a physical challenge and a cultural experience.
tips: Beautiful and old road that was used to join these towns between Cazorla and Úbeda and that over the years thousands of stories have passed. It remains a perfect path with views of the town of Torreperogil.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Torreperogil. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town to begin your jog.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally dog-friendly. However, as it follows a traditional livestock trail (Cañada Real), it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through areas where livestock might be present or near settlements.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summers can be hot in this region, so consider early morning or late afternoon jogs during those months. Winters are generally mild.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to jog this route. It follows public pathways, including sections of the historic Cañada Real.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of paved paths, making it largely accessible and suitable for jogging. While mostly accessible, some sections may require sure footing, especially if you venture off the main path.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy views of the town of Torreperogil and pass through areas known for their olive groves. The route follows the historic Cañada Real from Cazorla to Úbeda, offering a glimpse into an ancient pathway.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' for jogging. It covers 12.8 kilometers with an elevation gain of 130 meters. While not overly strenuous, it's best suited for individuals with good physical fitness who are comfortable with this distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Can I use this path for activities other than jogging?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! While ideal for jogging, the path is also commonly used for other outdoor activities such as hiking, cycling, and mountain biking, offering a versatile experience for various users.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the Cañada Real section?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the Cañada Real from Cazorla to Úbeda, an ancient pathway that historically connected these towns. It's a traditional livestock trail, rich in history, and has seen 'thousands of stories' over the years.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of several official trails, including the Cañada Real El Paso, Calle El Quejical, and the Cañada Real de Cazorla-Úbeda.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly jogging route?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length of 12.8 kilometers, it might be challenging for very young children. However, families with older children who are accustomed to jogging or longer walks could find it enjoyable. The paved sections make it accessible.

waypoints.name: Cañada Real from Cazorla to Úbeda
